name: Jan.ai
pricing_notes: Fully open source (AGPL-3.0); no API costs; hardware is the cost

auth.notes: Local server; no auth by default; remote access requires manual configuration

performance.notes: Local inference; performance depends on hardware; llama.cpp backend

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source desktop LLM app similar to LM Studio. OpenAI-compatible local API. Cross-platform (Mac/Windows/Linux). llama.cpp inference backend. Plugin system for extensions. JanServer for headless mode (experimental). Less polished UI than LM Studio but fully open source (AGPL). Smaller community than Ollama.

gotchas: AGPL license may require open-sourcing modifications in some contexts
Less polished than LM Studio; UI can be buggy
Smaller community and slower development velocity than Ollama/LM Studio
Headless JanServer is experimental — not production-ready
best_when: Users needing fully open-source (AGPL) local LLM desktop app
Privacy-first teams that can't use LM Studio (proprietary backend)
Users wanting extensible plugin architecture for custom features
Quick local testing of GGUF models with GUI
avoid_when: Best local LLM experience (LM Studio is more polished)
Applications where AGPL licensing creates legal concerns
summary: Jan.ai is a fully open-source (AGPL) desktop app for running LLMs locally with OpenAI-compatible API. Cross-platform Mac/Windows/Linux with plugin system. Uses llama.cpp backend. Best for users requiring fully open-source local LLM tooling. Less polished than LM Studio; smaller community than Ollama. Not recommended for headless/production use.
